sections.title: A Missing Fox
Talking to Guildmaster Apatura
Talking to Apatura again

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Nilsal, hunter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nilsal, Apatura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you free to talk about my quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Absolutely. Have you spoken to Verity yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not quite yet.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: If you're having a hard time finding her, she's behind the bar in the Burrow. Head down the stairs in the big tree. You can't miss it, and if you can, maybe you're not cut out for the Hunter Guild.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Sorry, but you need to be a better hunter before we can help you out.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Oh, you're a new face around here, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am indeed. Just exploring the savannah.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Well, I'm Apatura. Let me welcome you to our small corner of Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: We're mostly here to keep an eye on the local animal populations, and provide animal parts to the city.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: As for me personally, I'm the current master of this guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Current master?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: A hunter's work is dangerous. We tend to go through members faster than other guilds. That said, I'm usually too busy with politics and paperwork to go out hunting these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I hunt with the guild?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Absolutely! There's always more work to be done. Head down into the Burrow and talk to the other hunters to get an idea of what we need help with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: I'm not sure if you have the time or the patience, iknami, but a little birdy told me that our esteemed scribe Verity may require some assistance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of assistance?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: If I had to guess, she needs someone to find something for her.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: She's a wonderful scribe, but can lose some strange things here and there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Point me in her direction and I'll get cracking!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: She's behind the bar in the Burrow. Just look for the stressed woman trying to wrangle a bunch of barely- bathed hunters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I have the time for that right now.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Tetamo... Well, hopefully it's not too important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be off.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Scribe Verity
Talking to Verity again

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Have you spoken to Wolf yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll have a chat to her now.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Oh, hello there newcomer. I'm Guild Scribe Verity. Do you need me to explain the work we do here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: So it's nice and simple. Just chat to any of the hunters in here to help them out. They're all following reports that I've split up amongst them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reports?

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Indeed. There are all sorts of interesting creatures to hunt out there. Sometimes we get reports of a particularly unique example. When that happens, one of the hunters will set out to find it.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: If you do decide to take a job from any of the hunters, they'll tell you what type of creature they're looking into. Hunt that creature until you encounter the unique specimen.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Once you've caught it, bring some proof back to the hunter that sent you out in the first place. I'm sure they'll reward you for your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Now, what else can I do for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well actually, I heard there might be something I could help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Verity. I heard there might be something I could help you with?

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: You'll have to be slightly more specific. There's always something that needs doing down here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something a little birdy may have told Apatura?

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Tetamo... Pitri needs to stop twittering with the birds. Yes, Gilman and I are concerned about Fox. He left quietly in the night, but we have no record of what he was doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you normally keep track of the hunters here?

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: We try to, but it's like herding cats! We at least like to keep an eye on what they're hunting and point them in the right direction. It prevents us from losing track of too many creatures... or people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could anyone else have put Fox onto a hunt?

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: If anyone did, it would've been Wolf. She's always getting Fox into some kind of trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll follow up with her then.

sections.title: Talking to Guildmaster Apatura after talking to Verity

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Nilsal, hunter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nilsal, Apatura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you free to talk about my quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Absolutely. So was our little birdy right about Verity needing a hand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were... It wasn't an actual bird was it?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Of course not. Only Pitri talks to the birds.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Hunter Wolf
Talking to Wolf again

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Have you checked Kiko's bed yet? It's just to the side of this table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Could I grab that mouse to distract him with?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll get to it now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Do you happen to have that mouse to distract him with?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: I already gave it to you. If you've lost it, you'll need to get another for yourself. I'd do so quickly if I were you. Kiko won't be happy if he finds out it's gone.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: So, here to ask me what trouble I've gotten Fox into, are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not quite how I'd word it... but how did you know?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: I've honed my instincts over the years.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Well, that and the fact you had that whole conversation within earshot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So did you send Fox out to do something?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Sort of. He's on some very unofficial business for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How unofficial?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: If I told you... I'd have to kill you!

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Your face! Don't worry, I'm just pulling your leg.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: I asked him to look into making some repairs to Kiko's bed. It's been getting tatty lately. I'm guessing he didn't end up having the materials needed. That'll be why he headed out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I guess the first step is to work out what he went to get. Could I take a look at Kiko's bed?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: You're welcome to try, but you'll need to convince Kiko to let you. He's just by the table there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any advice for convincing him?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: He's very easy to distract. We have a little fake mouse we use sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I borrow it when I have a free hand to take it?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Sure thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I borrow it to have a go at distracting him?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Absolutely!

sections.title: Petting Kiko

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Kiko
lines.text: Can you scratch my belly next? It's perfectly safe.

sections.title: Using an unwound toy mouse on Kiko

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Kiko
lines.text: Are you not going to wind it up first? Come on now, put some effort in.

sections.title: Checking the cat bed

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Kiko
lines.text: Do you mind? I'm trying to sleep here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go, little guy.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Kiko
lines.text: A mouse? Fantastic!

sections.title: Using a wound toy mouse on Kiko or the cat bed
Checking the bed again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go, little guy.

sections.title: Talking to Wolf after inspecting the cat bed
Talking to Wolf again

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Have you found Fox?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't yet. You said he might be hunting pyre foxes, right?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: That's right. They're about halfway between here and the Locus Oasis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll head over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to have a look at that bed. I've never seen such luxury material for a cat bed before.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Only the best for our Kiko.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing it's the actual fur itself that Fox will be looking to replace. It looks like he cut a small bit off. I don't suppose you know where he'd have gone to get that.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: We have a contact who lives in Outer Fortis. She stocks the best of the best when it comes to fabrics. That'll be where he went, but knowing Fox, he won't have gone directly there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: He'll have stopped off for a bit of hunting first. There are some pyre foxes about halfway between here and the Locus Oasis that he was eyeing up. I bet that's where you'll find him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, I'd better get out there then.

sections.title: Talking to Verity after speaking to Wolf

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems like Fox is trying to find something to repair Kiko's bed with.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Why does that not surprise me? There's two things that man loves: that cat, and not filing in his reports correctly.

sections.title: Talking to Guildmaster Apatura after checking the cat bed

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Nilsal, hunter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nilsal, Apatura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you free to talk about my quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I checked in with Verity and apparently Fox has gone missing.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Ha, that certainly sounds like Fox! What errand has Wolf got him running this time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know Wolf was involved?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Let's just say you don't run things around here without good instincts. Let me guess - it's also got something to do with Kiko.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's spot on! How did you know?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: It's an open secret how much Fox and Wolf care about that cat. We all do of course, but those two don't half spoil him rotten.

sections.title: Fox Tracking
Talking to Guild Hunter Fox
Talking to Fox again

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: How's that poul... pool... poultice... Did you find the plants?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've got them both.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: It doesn't look like you do... Who's the one with the head wound again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find them?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: It's a bit fuzzy... I've lost a lot of blood! One should be around here... the other may be near the oasis... with the capybabas... haha, capybaras...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Fox? Everyone's worried about you.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Something attacked me in the dark... I can't move much...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help you? Maybe patch you up?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: That would be much appreciated! There should be a couple of plants around to make a poultice... Hold it together with an embertailed jerboa tail, and...

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: There should be a couple of plants around to make a... make a...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stay with me, Fox! Where are these plants?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: It's a bit fuzzy... I've lost a lot of blood! One should be around here... the other may be near the oasis... with the capybabas... haha, capybaras...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what am I making with them?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: A poultice! And hold it together with the tail from an embertailed jerboa.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Plants. Tail. Poultice. On it.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Don't forget... your traps for the jerboa... they're boxy...

sections.title: Talking to Guildmaster Apatura after finding Guild Hunter Fox

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Nilsal, hunter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nilsal, Apatura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you free to talk about my quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fox has been injured out in the savannah!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Well, that is impressive. How has he managed that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said he was attacked by an unknown assailant.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Well as you're not offering your condolences, I can only assume he's survived the encounter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He should be fine once I help him make a poultice for the wound.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Excellent. A final question - was his hand injured?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Wonderful! Then I look foward to reading his report.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Hunter Wolf after talking to Fox

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found Fox. He's been injured by something out in the savannah.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Oh no... I should've offered to go with him. It's never sensible to be out after dark alone...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to do what I can to make sure he's okay.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Well thanks for checking in at least. He's still breathing and that's all Fox needs to come out on top.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Scribe Verity after talking to Fox

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Any updates on Fox?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's been injured out in the savannah, but I'll do my best to make sure he makes it back.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Tetamo! Only he could get injured on an errand for a cat!

sections.title: Picking the leafy bush

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I need any more leaves from this bush.

sections.title: Picking the rough-looking bush

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I need any more leaves from this bush.

sections.title: Using two poultice ingredients together without the third
Using two poultice ingredients together while holding all three
Talking to Fox with the makeshift poultice

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that poultice here for you.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Kuani! That's making a difference already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Happy to help. Now, how did you get on finding what you needed to fix Kiko's bed?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: I thought a little detour wouldn't hurt. I'd caught a glimpse of the most stunning pelt ever seen!

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: As I was tracking it, I was attacked and knocked out. When I came to, it was gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gone?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Without a trace, as well as most of my hunting equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think someone attacked you for it, or was it just an opportunist looting you?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: I was unconscious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, of course.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: You've already done me a huge favour, but I don't suppose I could ask for one more small one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I could. Especially if it goes towards repairing that cat's bed and getting you back to the guild.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Well it's your lucky day! Make some room and I'll give you the sample needed to fix the bed.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Well it's your lucky day! Take this sample to a woman called Atza in Outer Fortis. She'll have exactly what we need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where in the outskirts can I find her? And... what exactly do we need, again?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: She lives by the south western gate of the city. She'll be able to give us the matching fur to repair the cat's bed with.

sections.title: Talking to Fox after giving him the poultice

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: I thought a little detour wouldn't hurt. I'd caught a glimpse of the most stunning pelt ever seen!

sections.title: Talking to Fox before getting the fur sample with inventory space
Talking to Fox again

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Any success with Atza yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: If you're struggling to find her, she's in Outer Fortis, near the south western gate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I appear to have lost the sample you gave me...

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Luckily, I always come prepared. Here's a spare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah. It seems I don't have room for it.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Well I'm not going anywhere. Let me know when you do have room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you've got another sample of Kiko's bed for me to take to Atza?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Fortunately for you, I'm always prepared... And our thief clearly didn't care for fine fur samples.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's convenient! Usually I'm the one that's always ready for these situations.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Perfect! Here you go!

sections.title: Talking to Guildmaster Apatura after giving Guild Hunter Fox the poultice

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Nilsal, hunter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nilsal, Apatura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you free to talk about my quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Absolutely. How's it going, iknami?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're getting there. I should have everything sorted very soon.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Hunter Wolf after giving Fox the poultice

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: How's the Fox situation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's doing well. He's got me doing a few things for him whilst he recuperates.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Ha! Now that's the Fox I know. I'll have to give him some odd jobs once he's back to make up for ones you're covering!

sections.title: Talking to Guild Scribe Verity after giving Fox the poultice

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Any updates on Fox?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it, but I'm getting there.

sections.title: Talking to Atza

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. You must be Atza. I heard you're the person to talk to about getting some material to match a sample?

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: I might be. Depends who's asking... and what you're looking for, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your friend Fox was going to bring this sample to you, but he's been a little... waylaid.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Well, this wouldn't be the first time! Let's take a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't seem to have it with me.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: You'll have to go back to Fox and grab it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Good, good. Luckily for you, I recognise this fur. I do have a bit left, but I'll have to dig it out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I just wait here then?

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Oh... Fox usually helps me with my chores when he visits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's funny. He didn't mention that to me.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: That's alright, I'm sure a capable adventurer like you will manage fine. Fox was going to assemble some equipment for me. You can manage that, can't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose so. Where do I start?

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: It's all in that pile on the floor there. You can work it out, I'm sure.

sections.title: Talking to Atza after giving her the fur

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Now, luckily for you, I recognise this fur. I do have a bit left, but I'll have to dig it out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I just wait here then?

sections.title: Talking to Fox after giving Atza the material

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. I've given Atza the fur sample.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Did she give you the matching material?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I should check back in with her.

sections.title: Talking to Atza after she asks to set up the equipment

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Don't forget, I need a hand setting up that equipment on the floor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm on it.

sections.title: Setting up the pile of equipment without a hammer
Setting up the pile of equipment

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: So anyway, how did you come to be doing this for Fox?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, he actually got attacked on his way over here.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Shouldn't you be helping him heal up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh he'll be alright. He was on the mend when I left him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All done!

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Oh, that's looking much better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that's done, did you manage to find the material?

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Thanks for your help today. I hope that fur works for your project.

sections.title: Talking to Atza after setting up the equipment
Talking to Atza again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I get a replacement for that fur? I managed to lose it.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: You can, for now. I don't have an unlimited supply though, so be careful.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Ah, you don't have space for that.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: You should take that material back to Fox.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that's done, did you manage to find the material?

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Yes, but you don't have enough room for it! Come back when you have space.

lines.speaker: Atza
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

sections.title: Talking to Fox

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. I got the fur from Atza. She's certainly... something.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: She didn't make you do odd jobs for her, did she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: She does it every time I visit!

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Now, I'll need you to take this report back to Verity, but it looks like you don't have space for it at the moment.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Anyway, now that's that, would you mind letting Wolf and Verity know I'm okay? Oh, and can you take my report back to the guild?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Please apologise to Verity for the condition it's in. Oh, and of course, don't forget to use that fur along with another embertailed jerboa tail to fix Kiko's bed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Want me to save the world while I'm at it?

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: No need! You've already saved my world by making Kiko happy.

sections.title: Reading Fox's report
Talking to Fox again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost your report.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: How is it you've lost the report and yet you somehow don't have space for another one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's just the adventuring way! If something isn't tied down, I'll grab it, and probably lose it at some point too!

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: You're lucky I don't have anywhere else to be. I'll write you another one. Just a moment...

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Hello there. Have you taken that report back to Verity yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I still need to get around to it.

sections.title: Finishing up
Talking to Verity

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Is that Fox's report? Thanks for bringing that back to us. How was he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's injured, but he's on the mend. I expect he'll be back in here soon enough.

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Good to hear. Once you're done with the cat's bed, you might want to check in with Guildmaster Apatura.

sections.title: Talking to Verity before repairing the bed

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Thanks for all your help. Once you're done with the cat's bed, you might want to check in with Guildmaster Apatura.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and speak to her now.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Hunter Wolf after turning in the report

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: How's the Fox situation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just about sorted. He'll be back here very soon.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Thank you, hunter.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Hunter Fox after turning in the report

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Fox
lines.text: Hello there. Have you taken that report back to Verity yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. She was glad to hear that you'll be back soon.

sections.title: Repairing Kiko's bed

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Kiko
lines.text: Well as much as I resent the trickery used, this bed is much comfier now.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Scribe Verity after repairing the bed

lines.speaker: Guild Scribe Verity
lines.text: Thanks for all your help. Have you checked in with the Guildmaster?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and speak to her now.

sections.title: Talking to Guild Hunter Wolf after repairing the bed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like the Fox situation is all sorted.

lines.speaker: Guild Hunter Wolf (Master)
lines.text: Thank you, hunter. I'm not sure I could have forgiven myself if something terrible had happened to him.

sections.title: Talking to Apatura

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everything's all wrapped up!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Kuani! So what was so important for our darling Kiko that poor Fox risked life and limb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, basically Wolf sent Fox to get fur to fix Kiko's bed, but he actually needed to talk to Atza, and Atza needed me to...

lines.speaker: Guildmaster Apatura
lines.text: Actually... I think I'll just read the report.
